title: Self-illuminating turret cover
abstract: A turret cover for a turret assembly. In one or more embodiments the turret cover includes an upper portion including a turret housing defining a battery recess and an illumination recess. An illumination assembly can be mounted in the illumination recess. In various embodiments, the illumination assembly includes a downwardly facing surface including a lighting element. In various embodiments, the turret cover includes a lower portion including an exterior sleeve portion of an at least partially transparent plastic material and a plurality of etched adjustment marks. In some embodiments, the lower portion is at least partially inserted into the illumination recess such that an upwardly facing edge of the exterior sleeve portion is positioned adjacent to the lighting element of the illumination assembly such that, in operation, the lighting element illuminates the plurality of etched adjustment marks via an edge lighting process.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A turret cover for a turret assembly on a firearm aiming device, the turret cover comprising: an upper portion including a turret housing defining a battery recess and an illumination recess; a battery assembly mounted in the battery recess; an illumination assembly mounted in the illumination recess and electrically connected to the battery assembly, the illumination assembly including a lighting element, and a lower portion including an exterior sleeve portion of an at least partially transparent plastic material, the exterior sleeve portion including a plurality of adjustment marks etched into the at least partially transparent material, the lower portion at least partially inserted into the illumination recess such that an upwardly facing edge of the exterior sleeve portion is positioned adjacent to the lighting element of the illumination assembly; wherein, in operation, the lighting element of the illumination assembly is powered on by the battery assembly and illuminates, via an edge-lighting process, the plurality of etched adjustment marks, 2. The turret cover of claim 1, wherein the turret housing has a sidewall extending from a lower end to a top surface, the lower end of the sidewall defining a first opening in the turret housing to a downwardly facing interior surface, the downwardly facing interior surface and a first interior surface of the sidewall defining the illumination recess. 3. The turret cover of claim 2, wherein the top surface of the turret housing defines a second opening in the turret housing to an upwardly facing interior surface, the upwardly facing interior surface and a second interior surface of the sidewall defining the batter}' recess. 4. The turret cover of claim 2, wherein the illumination assembly includes a circuit board having an upwardly facing surface positioned adjacent to the downwardly facing interior surface and the circuit board having a downwardly facing surface including the lighting element 5. The turret cover as in any one of claims 2-4, wherein the exterior sleeve portion has a sidewall extending from a lower end to an upper end, the sidewall defining a first opening at the upper end and a second opening at the lower end, the upper end of the sidewall including an inwardly extending flange portion at the upper end. 6. The turret cover of claim 5, wherein the lower portion is at least partially inserted into the illumination recess and fastened to the upper portion such that an upwardly facing edge at the upper end of the exterior sleeve portion is positioned adjacent to the lighting element of the illumination assembly. 7. The turret cover of claim 5, wherein the exterior sleeve portion surrounds an interior backing portion having a sidewall that extends from a lower end to an upper end, the interior backing portion including a circumferential lip, at the upper end, defined by an inwardly extending circumferential recess in the sidewall, the interior backing portion upwardly insertable into the exterior sleeve portion via the second opening such that the flange portion of the exterior sleeve portion and the circumferential l ip fit together to limit upward insertion of the interior backing portion. 8. The turret cover of claim 7, wherein the interior backing portion is constructed from a non-transparent material. 9. The turret cover as in any one of claims 1-8, wherein: the exterior sleeve portion has a painted interior surface such that the interior surface is substantially non-transparent. 10. The turret cover as in any one of claims 1-9, wherein: the illumination assembly further includes a switch configured to control output of the lighting element; and the turret housing includes a push button configured to interact with the switch in the illumination recess. 1 1. The turret cover of claim 10, wherein: the switch is configured to toggle the output of the lighting element between at least three different outputs. 12. The turret cover as in any one of claims 1-1 1, wherein the exterior sleeve portion is constructed from at least one of acrylic and polycarbonate, 13. The turret cover as in any one of claims 1-12, wherein the plurality of adjustment marks are painted using a reflective material paint. 14. A firearm aiming device comprising: a scope body including an interior erector assembly and reticle ceil; a turret assembly mounted on the scope body, the turret assembly including an interior mechanical portion configured to engage the erector assembly within the scope body for aiming adjustments, the turret assembly further including a turret cover enclosing the interior mechanical portion, the turret cover including: 

 an upper portion including a turret housing defining a battery recess and an illumination recess: a battety assembly mounted in the batter}' recess; an illumination assembly mounted in the illumination recess and electrically connected to the battery assembly, the illumination assembly including a lighting element, and a lower portion including an exterior sleeve portion of an at least partially transparent plastic material, the exterior sleeve portion including a plurality of adjustment marks etched into the at least partially transparent material, the lower portion at least partially inserted into the illumination recess such that an upwardly facing edge of the exterior sleeve portion is positioned adjacent to the lighting element of the illumination assembly, wherein, in operation, the lighting element of the illumination assembly is powered on by the battery assembly and illuminates, via an edge lighting process, the plurality of etched adjustment marks. 15. The turret cover of claim 14, wherein the turret housing has a sidewail extending from a lower end to a top surface, the lower end of the sidewail defining a first opening in the turret housing to a downwardly facing interior surface, the downwardly facing interior surface and a first interior surface of the sidewail defining the illumination recess. 16, The turret cover of claim 15, wherein the illumination assembly includes a circuit board having an upwardly facing surface positioned adjacent to the downwardly facing interior surface and the circuit board having a downwardly facing surface including the lighting element 17. The turret cover as in any one of claims 15-16, wherein the exterior sleeve portion has a sidewall extending from a lower end to an upper end, the sidewall defining a first opening at the upper end and a second opening at the lower end, the upper end of the sidewall including an inwardly extending flange portion at the upper end; and the lower portion is at least partially inserted into the illumination recess and fastened to the upper portion such that an upwardly facing edge at the upper end of the exterior sleeve portion is positioned adjacent to the lighting element of the illumination assembly. 18. The turret cover of claim 17, wherein the exterior sleeve portion surrounds an interior backing portion having a sidewall that extends from a lower end to an upper end, the interior backing portion including a ci cumferential lip, at the upper end, defined by an inwardly extending circumferential recess in the sidewall, the interior backing portion upwardly insertable into the exterior sleeve portion via the second opening such that the flange portion of the exterior sleeve portion and the circumferential lip fit together to limit upward insertion of the interior backing portion. 19. A turret cover for a turret assembly on a firearm aiming device, the turret cover comprising; an upper portion including a turret housing having a sidewall extending from a lower end to a top surface, the lower end of the sidewall defining a first opening in the turret housing to a downwardly facing interior surface, the downwardly facing interior surface and a first interior surface of the sidewall defining an illumination recess, the top surface of the turret housing defining a second opening in the turret housing to an upwardly facing interior surface, the 

 upwardly facing interior surface and a second interior surface of the sidewall defining a battery recess; a battery assembly mounted in the battery recess; an illumination assembly mounted in the illumination recess and electrically connected to the battery assembly, the illumination assembly including a circuit board having an upwardly facing surface positioned adjacent to the downwardly facing interior surface and the circuit board having a downwardly facing surface including a plurality of lighting elements; and a lower portion including an exterior sleeve portion of an at least partially transparent plastic material surrounding an interior backing portion, the exterior sleeve portion including a plurality of adjustment marks etched into the at least partially transparent material, the lower portion at least partially inserted into the illumination recess and fastened to the upper portion such that an upwardly facing edge of the exterior sleeve portion is positioned adjacent to the lighting element of the illumination assembly; wherein, in operation, the lighting element of the illumination assembly is powered on by the battery assembly and illuminates, via an edge lighting process, the plurality of etched adjustment marks, 20. The turret cover of claim 19, wherein: the exterior sleeve portion has a sidewall extending from a lower end to an upper end, the sidewall defining a first opening at the upper end and a second opening at the lower end, the upper end of the sidewall including an inwardly extending flange portion at the upper end; and 

 the interior backing portion has a sidewall that extends from a lower end to an upper end, the interior backing portion including an upwardly facing surface and a circumferential lip at the upper end, the circumferential lip defined by an inwardly extending circumferential recess in the sidewall; and the interior backing portion is upwardly insertable into the exterior sleeve portion via the second opening such that the flange portion of the exterior sleeve portion circumferential lip fit together to limit upward insertion of the interior backing portion. 21. The turret cover of claim 19, wherein the plurality of adjustment marks are painted. 22. The turret cover of claim 21, wherein the plurality of adjustment marks are painted using a reflective material paint. 23. A turret assembly for a firearm aiming device including a scope body and an interior erector assembly and reticle cell, the turret assembly comprising: an interior mechanical portion configured to engage the erector assembly within the scope body for aiming adjustments; and a turret cover rotatably mounted to and enclosing the interior mechanical portion, the turret cover including: an upper portion including a turret housing having a sidewall extending from a lower end to a top surface, the lower end of the sidewall defining a first opening in the turret housing to a downwardly facing interior surface, the downwardly facing interior surface and a first interior surface of the sidewall defining an illumination recess, the top surface of the turret housing defining a second opening in the turret housing to an upwardly facing interior surface, the upwardly facing interior surface and a second interior surface of the sidewall defining a battery recess; 

 a batte assembly mounted in the batter}- recess; an illumination assembly mounted in the illumination recess and electrically connected to the battery assembly, the illumination assembly including a circuit board having an upwardly facing surface positioned adjacent to the downwardly facing interior surface and the circuit board having a downwardly facing surface including a lighting element; and a lower portion including an exterior sleeve portion of an at least partially transparent plastic material surrounding an interior backing portion, the exterior sleeve portion including a plurality of adjustment marks etched into the at least partially transparent material, the lower portion at least partially inserted into the illumination recess and fastened to the upper portion such that an upwardly facing edge of the exterior sleeve portion is positioned adjacent to the lighting element of the illumination assembly; wherein, in operation, the lighting element of the illumination assembly is powered on by the batten,' assembly and illuminates, via an edge lighting process, the plurality of etched adjustment marks. 24, The turret assembly of claim 23, wherein: the exterior sleeve portion has a sidewall extending from a lower end to an upper end, the sidewall defining a first opening at the upper end and a second opening at the lower end, the upper end of the sidewall including an inwardly extending flange portion at the upper end; and the interior backing portion has a sidewall that extends from a lower end to an upper end, the interior backing portion including an upwardly facing surface and a circumferential lip at the 

 upper end, the circumferential lip defined by an inwardly extending circumferential recess in the sidewali; and the interior backing portion is upwardly insertable into the exterior sleeve portion via the second opening such that the flange portion of the exterior sleeve portion circumferential lip fit together to limit upward insertion of the interior backing portion. 25. The turret assembly as in any one of claims 23-24, wherein: the illumination assembly further includes a switch configured to control output of the lighting element; and the top surface includes a push button configured to interact with the switch in the illumination recess. 26. The turret assembly of claim 25, wherein: the switch is configured to toggl e the output of the lighting element between at least three different outputs. 27. The turret assembly as in any one of claims 23-26, wherein: the exterior sleeve portion has a painted interior surface such that the interior surface is substantially non-transparent. 28. A system for illumination of a turret assembly on a firearm aiming device, the system comprising: a turret cover, including: an upper portion including a turret housing defining an illumination recess, 

 an illumination assembly mounted in the illumination recess and electrically connected to the batten,' assembly, the illumination assembly including a lighting element; and a lower portion including an exterior sleeve portion of an at least partially transparent plastic material, the exterior sleeve portion including a plurality of adjustment marks etched into the at least partially transparent material, the lower portion at least partially inserted into the illumination recess such that an upwardly facing edge of the exterior sleeve portion is positioned adjacent to the lighting element of the illumination assembly; and a battery assembly electrically coupled to the illumination assembly; wherein, in operation, the lighting element of the illumination assembly is powered on by the battery assembly and illuminates, via an edge lighting process, the plurality of etched adjustment marks. 29. The system of claim 28, wherein: the battery assembly is housed within a firearm aiming device including a scope body and an interior erector assembly and reticle ceil. 30. The system as in any one of claims 28-29, further comprising: a solar array, including a plurality of solar panels electrically connected to the battery assembly. 31. A turret cover for a turret assembly on a firearm aiming device, the turret cover comprising: an upper portion including a turret housing defining a battery recess and an illumination recess; a battery assembly mounted in the battery recess; an illumination assembly mounted in the illumination recess and electrically connected to the battery assembly, the illumination assembly including a lighting element, and a lower portion including an exterior sleeve portion having a sidewall extending from a lower end to an upper end and defining a first opening at the upper end and a second opening at the lower end, the sidewall including a plurality of optical fibers, each of the plurality of optical fibers positioned arranged at least partially within the sidewall extending between the upper end and lower end of the sidewall, each of the plurality of optical fibers having an exposed first end at an upwardly facing edge of the upper end of the sidewall, the sidewall including a plurality of adjustment marks spaced circumferentially about an exterior surface of the sidewall, the lower portion at least partially inserted into the illumination recess such that the upwardly facing edge is positioned adjacent to the lighting element of the illumination assembly; wherein, in operation, the lighting element of the illumination assembly is powered on by the battery assembly and illuminates, via the plurality of optical fibers, the plurality of adjustment marks. 32. The turret cover of claim 31, wherein the exterior sleeve portion is constructed of an at least partially transparent plastic material. 33. The turret cover of claim 32, wherein the plurality of adjustment marks are etched into the at least partially transparent material of the exterior sleeve portion. 34. The turret cover as in any one of claims 31-33, wherein the sidewall includes a plurality of recesses in the exterior of the sidewall that expose a portion of each of the plurality of optical fibers, wherein the plurality of adjustment marks are defined by the portions of the plurality of optical fibers exposed by the plurality of recesses. 35. The turret cover as in any one of claims 31-34, wherein each of the plurality of optical fibers are arranged spaced circumferential!}' about the sidewall and corresponding with one or more of the plurality of adjustment marks.
